data.title: These Analysts Lower Their Forecasts On Gambling.com Following Q4 Earnings
data.description: Gambling.com Group Limited (NASDAQ: GAMB) exceeded expectations in Q4 with earnings per share of 30 cents, surpassing the analyst estimate of 24 cents. Quarterly sales amounted to $46.236 million, higher than the anticipated $46.057 million. For FY2026, the company anticipates sales between $170.00 million and $180.00 million, contrasting with market projections of $185.309 million. CEO and Co-Founder Charles Gillespie stated, "We achieved record Q4 revenue and Adjusted EBITDA, with revenue climbing 31% year-over-year to $46.2 million and Adjusted EBITDA up 5% to $15.5 million. The sports data services segment notably expanded by 29% quarterly, constituting 26% of total revenue."

data.title: Obstacle for Fed Chair pick as Justice Dept. threat complicates Fed investigation with possible appeal for Kevin Warsh.
data.description: A probe into the activities of the Federal Reserve hit a roadblock on Friday, potentially hindering progress. However, an appeal by the department could further impede Kevin Warsh's actions.

data.title: Switching addresses resulted in losing Medicare coverage for one woman, raising concerns about the impact of using a post office box.
data.description: A woman's health insurance was jeopardized due to using a post office box. This unexpected consequence highlights a concerning issue in the system.

data.title: LINK price forecast sees $10.50-$12.00 by April 2026, signaling 15-32% upside from $9.01. Analysts bullish on Chainlink.
data.description: Analysts are optimistic about the bullish potential of Chainlink (LINK), predicting a target range of $10.50 to $12.00 in the near future. The current technical analysis indicates a potential upside of 15-32% from the current $9.01 level.

data.title: Regional rivalries and geography hinder efforts to address oil disruption in the Strait of Hormuz amid Iran conflict.
data.description: Geographical factors and rivalries among regions have hindered Gulf countries from identifying a viable substitute for the strait. The ongoing conflict with Iran has led to the closure of the strait, significantly impacting transportation routes for vital resources.

data.title: US's reliance on oil has reduced thanks to energy efficiency and renewables, driving down household budgets.
data.description: Gasoline continues to play a significant role in household budgets, but advancements in energy efficiency and increased use of renewables have led to a decrease in the economy's overall dependence on petroleum. This shift has resulted in a notable reduction in the consumption of gasoline, highlighting a move towards more sustainable energy sources. This change is reflected in key data points such as declining petroleum usage in various sectors and an increase in the adoption of cleaner energy alternatives. As a result, households and industries are becoming less reliant on gasoline as technology and renewable energy sources continue to reshape the energy landscape.

data.title: Concerns rise as Wall Street insiders warn about the $3 trillion private credit market, likening it to 'cockroaches'.
data.description: Financial experts on Wall Street are using vivid language, such as referencing the despised insect, to sound the alarm about the $3 trillion high-risk lending sector.

data.title: Is your Apple Watch now your work boss? Wearable tech data is changing how people work – but is it helpful? #Productivity #AppleWatch
data.description: The increasing availability of data gathered from wearable technology is providing individuals with insights on how to enhance their job performance. This raises the question of whether this development is beneficial or not.

data.title: Jeff Bezos, billionaire owner of a newspaper, aims to double newsroom productivity with half the budget due to years of losses.
data.description: The wealthy proprietor of a newspaper, frustrated by continuous losses over the years, aims to increase productivity in the newsroom twofold while cutting the budget by half.

data.title: Inflow streak of 5 days continues for spot Bitcoin ETFs in 2026, gathering $767 million in the US.
data.description: Over the course of five consecutive days, US spot Bitcoin ETFs experienced inflows totaling approximately $767 million, marking the first instance of such substantial inflows this year.

data.title: Ollie's Bargain Outlet Analysts Slash Their Forecasts After Q4 Results
data.description: Ollie's Bargain Outlet Holdings, Inc. posted fourth-quarter earnings in line with expectations, reporting $1.39 per share. Despite a 16.8% year-over-year increase in quarterly sales to $779.256 million, this missed the projected $783.271 million. President and CEO Eric van der Valk noted that strong comp growth, good margins, and disciplined expense management drove better-than-expected results. The company forecasts fiscal 2026 adjusted earnings between $4.40 and $4.50 per share, slightly below the $4.48 estimate. Anticipated fiscal 2026 sales range from $2.985 billion to $3.013 billion. More details on the news available but not disclosed here.
These Analysts Cut Their Forecasts On Dick's Sporting Following Q4 Earnings

data.title: Biomea Fusion Presents Phase II COVALENT-111 Data in Type 2 Diabetes at the 19th International Conference on Advanced Technologies & Treatments for Diabetes 2026
data.description: On March 14, 2026, San Carlos-based company Biomea Fusion, Inc. disclosed that Dr. Juan Pablo Frías presented at the 19th International Conference on Advanced Technologies & Treatments for Diabetes in Barcelona. The presentation detailed encouraging outcomes from the Phase II COVALENT-111 study of icovamenib in type 2 diabetes patients, following a 52-week assessment. Biomea Fusion, listed as BMEA on Nasdaq, focuses on diabetes and obesity and is deemed a clinical-stage entity. The presentation aimed to showcase the positive findings regarding the efficacy, safety, and tolerability of icovamenib in treating T2D.
